title: The last time something good arrived
subtitle: A journal prompt set for finding evidence that you have already received more than you once thought possible.
excerpt: A journal prompt set from Day 27 of the MONEY program. Use to recall specific instances of receiving well, to build a case for your own capacity to hold incoming good.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 27 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and the Week 4 daily prompts of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448).

content.text: A journal prompt set from Day 27 of the MONEY program. The prompts build an evidence base: instances from your own life where you received something larger than you expected and held it without falling apart. Twenty minutes, no editing.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
What emotions surface when I imagine receiving a large amount of money at once?
Name them. No need to resolve them yet.
What previous experiences remind me that I've successfully received and managed more than I once thought possible?
Write specific instances. Money, opportunity, love, support, any form of receiving that surprised you.
The last time something unexpectedly good arrived, what happened next?
Write it as a story, not a reflection. What happened, in order.
What would it feel like if a large sum landed in my account today and I responded with calm instead of overwhelm?
Describe the body first. Then the thoughts. Then what you would do.
How can I prepare my energy now to hold big sums with ease when they arrive?
Write one practical thing and one internal thing.
Adapted from Day 27 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and the Week 4 daily prompts of The Money Journal (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
